title: Consumer ingest contract + sample importer (Ed-Fi LearningStandard + schema.org educationalAlignment)
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID primary / ASN URI optional). Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. PLAN.md v0.2 adds an M1 optimization: ship a tiny ingest contract + sample importer so a consuming partner's integration lift is near-zero — directly attacking the unsecured-partner / 'delivered, not merged' risk. The two target formats are Ed-Fi LearningStandard and schema.org educationalAlignment. confidence, coverage, and notEndorsement must survive into every export so downstream systems cannot silently treat reviewed proposals as authoritative fact.
objective: Build a documented ingest contract and sample importer for at least one consumer format (Ed-Fi LearningStandard and/or schema.org educationalAlignment), preserving alignment provenance metadata (confidence, coverage, notEndorsement) in the consumer record.
acceptanceCriteria: Ingest contract document specifies: the canonical field mapping from AlignmentRecord (and StandardAssociation where applicable) to the consumer format, the minimum required fields a consumer must accept, and how confidence/coverage/notEndorsement are carried so a downstream system cannot silently treat a reviewed proposal as authoritative fact.

Sample importer for Ed-Fi LearningStandard and/or schema.org educationalAlignment reads canonical AlignmentRecord JSON and emits a valid consumer-format record; at least one of the two formats is fully implemented.
Emitted records validated against the target format schema (Ed-Fi or schema.org); test fixtures committed (canonical record → expected consumer-format output) and diffed in CI.
notEndorsement flag and aiConfidence are preserved in the consumer output; the importer must not drop or silently flatten them.
Code MIT; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint green; commit is DCO signed-off; no credentials or secrets committed.
resources: PLAN.md (v0.2 — ingest contract + sample importer M1 optimization, confidence/notEndorsement in every export)
